Line 1006 also says "via the plugin-root .mcp.json", now .claude/mcp.json.

## Impact

Minimal - the doc carries a "Status: Implemented in 444a84c" header marking the body as a historical record. Noting it because the sibling spec WAS corrected in-body for the dropped repo-meta tools, so the two docs are maintained to different standards.

## Fix

Either extend the Status header to note "tool names were later prefixed np_; see SKILL.md for the shipped surface", or stop in-body corrections on the spec too. Pick one convention.

## Resolution
Status header extended to note the np_ prefix rename and that SKILL.md is the authority for the shipped surface; body left as a historical record
title: Plan doc's proposed README text lists the pre-rename, unprefixed tool names

body:
## Problem

955fac3 "feat: add write-surgical-code rule from Karpathy's LLM-coding guidelines (#61)" touches only .claude/rules/write-surgical-code.md, .gitignore and CLAUDE.md. The repo's own rule assigns exactly that file set to a no-bump type.

## Evidence

`git show --stat 955fac3` - .claude/rules/write-surgical-code.md | 46 ++, .gitignore | 6 ++-, CLAUDE.md | 2 +-. No path under skills/. .claude/rules/commit-types.md: "docs: / chore: / refactor: - changes confined to .claude/rules/, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, ... No path under skills/ changed, so no installed consumer sees a difference and no version bump is warranted."

## Impact

Low in effect - genuine feat: commits already justify the next minor so this does not change the computed version. It is a consistency gap: the same rule's 1.7.0 mis-type was corrected in CHANGELOG.md on this branch while this one was not.

## Fix

Per commit-types.md ("a wrong type that has already landed is corrected in the CHANGELOG rather than by rewriting history"), add a correction note to the release entry carrying 955fac3, matching the 1.7.0 block's format.

## Resolution
correction block added to CHANGELOG under Unreleased (git tag --contains confirms 955fac3 is unreleased), matching the existing 1.7.0 correction format
title: 955fac3 used feat: for a change confined to .claude/rules/ and CLAUDE.md

body:
## Problem

a7aa066 "feat: graphify integration, vendored-skill validator support, and tool hardening (#63)" bundles (1) vendoring a third-party skill, (2) a new validator allowlist + rule, and (3) hardening of three shipped scripts - 25 files, +1780/-30. Each lands independently.

## Evidence

`git show --stat a7aa066` - .claude/skills/graphify/** (9 files), scripts/validate-skill.py | 37 +-, skills/nitpicker/scripts/{check-rules-anatomy,fetch-pr-comments,process-sarif}.py, .claude/rules/vendored-skills.md | 33 +. commit-types.md: "Split the commit when the parts can land separately - the release signal this repo depends on degrades once multi-concern fix: commits become normal."

## Impact

The missing graphify LICENSE/NOTICE is precisely what a single-concern "vendor graphify" commit would have surfaced in review; bundling it under a hardening headline is how it passed unnoticed.

## Fix

No history rewrite. Going forward, split vendoring from tooling changes; make the vendored-skill checklist in .claude/rules/vendored-skills.md a review gate on any commit adding a VENDORED_SKILLS entry.

## Resolution
correction block added noting a7aa066 bundled three separable concerns and that a single-concern vendoring commit would have surfaced the missing LICENSE/NOTICE
title: a7aa066 bundles three unrelated concerns in one feat: commit

body:
## Problem

Two of the last fifteen commits use ci:, a type absent from both the rule's "Which type" list and CLAUDE.md's Versioning table.

## Evidence

1c28e3c "ci: fix coderabbit path filter that skipped nitpicker reviews (#58)", 8225405 "ci: exclude generated CHANGELOG.md from pre-commit hooks (#56)". commit-types.md "Which type" enumerates only feat:, fix:, docs:/chore:/refactor:, feat!:. CLAUDE.md:70-76 Versioning table rows: feat:, fix:, feat!:/BREAKING CHANGE:, chore:, docs:, refactor:.

## Impact

Benign for versioning (release-please treats ci: as no-bump), but /nitpicker commits audits against these tables, so an established, correct practice reads as a violation.

## Fix

Add ci: to the no-bump group in .claude/rules/commit-types.md and as a row in the CLAUDE.md Versioning table.

## Resolution
ci: added to the no-bump group in commit-types.md and to the Versioning tables in CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md
title: ci: is used on main but appears in neither documented commit-type table

body:
## Problem

Both documents place content as direct children of <body>. <main> count is 0 in both (verified by regex `<main[ >]`). <footer> at body level does map to contentinfo, so the pages have exactly one landmark and it wraps only the footer.

## Evidence

```
0001-dispatch-pipeline.html:  <main> tags=0
dispatch-cheatsheet.html:     <main> tags=0
```

## Impact

Screen-reader users cannot jump to content via landmark navigation (rotor / D key), and the content region is not programmatically distinguishable from the footer. WCAG 2.2 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (Level A).

## Fix

In both files wrap everything between the .eyebrow paragraph and <footer> in <main>. No CSS change needed - main is display: block by default.

## Resolution
<main> wraps the content region in both pages with <footer> outside it; verified via tag-stack walk, exactly one main per file, zero nesting mismatches
title: Neither lesson page has a main landmark so all content sits in no region

body:
## Problem

WebKit removes the implicit list role from any list whose list-style computes to none. The five pipeline steps then announce as five loose paragraphs - VoiceOver no longer says "list, 5 items" or "3 of 5". The step numbers come from `content: counter(step)` in ::before (:88), which is CSS-generated and not a reliable substitute.

## Evidence

style.css:80 `ol.pipeline { counter-reset: step; list-style: none; padding-left: 0; }`, consumed by 0001-dispatch-pipeline.html:36 `<ol class="pipeline">` with 5 <li>.

## Impact

Sequence and count - the entire point of a numbered pipeline - are lost for VoiceOver users. WCAG 2.2 1.3.1 (Level A).

## Fix

Add role="list" to the element at 0001-dispatch-pipeline.html:36. Restores the role in WebKit, no-op elsewhere.

## Resolution
role="list" added to ol.pipeline; swept both files, it is the only list-style:none rule
title: ol.pipeline list-style none strips list semantics in Safari and VoiceOver

body:
## Problem

@media print sets `body { color: #000; background: #fff; }` but does not reset --muted or --accent. @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) still matches while printing in Chrome and Firefox, so the dark palette is painted onto forced white paper for every rule using a variable - footer, .source-note, th, .answer (--muted) and .callout .label (--accent).

## Evidence

Computed WCAG contrast against the forced #ffffff:
| Text | Colour | Ratio | Threshold | Result |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| --muted (dark) | #a6a29a | 2.54:1 | 4.5:1 | FAIL |
| --accent (dark) | #e0a3a3 | 2.11:1 | 4.5:1 | FAIL |
| --muted (light) | #5b5b5b | 6.79:1 | 4.5:1 | pass |
| --accent (light) | #7a2e2e | 9.30:1 | 4.5:1 | pass |
On-screen contrast is clean in both themes; all 12 text pairs checked, lowest 6.67:1. Print-only defect.

## Impact

A dark-mode user who prints the lesson (the CSS header states "prints well" as an explicit goal at :2) gets footer, table headers, source notes and recall answers at ~2.5:1 - illegible on paper and non-recoverable.

## Fix

Reset the palette inside the print block at :146:
```css
@media print {
  :root { --ink: #1a1a1a; --muted: #5b5b5b; --accent: #7a2e2e;
          --bg: #fff; --rule: #999; --code-bg: #f2f0ea; --callout-bg: #f5f2ea; }
  ...
}
```

## Resolution
print block resets all 8 dark-overridden custom properties to their light values. Recomputed against #ffffff: --muted 2.54:1 -> 6.79:1, --accent 2.11:1 -> 9.30:1
title: Print stylesheet forces a white background but never resets the dark-mode custom properties

body:
## Problem

Nine <th> elements across both pages, zero carry scope. For the three simple 2-3 column tables implicit column association is adequate. The "Adding a command" table is not simple - its second body column uses rowspan="2", exactly the case where header inference stops being reliable across AT implementations.

## Evidence

`<th scope=` = 0 of `<th>` = 6 in the cheatsheet; the irregular table is at :41 with `<td rowspan="2">`.

## Impact

Cell-to-header association may be announced incorrectly in that one table. Best practice under WCAG 1.3.1; not a clear-cut AA failure for the simple tables.

## Fix

Add scope="col" to the two <th> at :40 of dispatch-cheatsheet.html. Apply to the remaining tables for consistency; it costs nothing.

## Resolution
scope="col" added to all 9 <th> across both files, including the two above the rowspan=2 table
title: The rowspan table has no scope on its headers

body:
## Problem

Empty-ruleset scan and cross-reference against both HTML files found one empty ruleset and one class used nowhere.

## Evidence

style.css:151 - `details.recall[open] { }` - an empty ruleset; the trailing comment "/* answers print when opened */" documents native <details> behaviour requiring no CSS. style.css:155 - `.no-print { display: none; }` - the class no-print appears in neither HTML file.

## Impact

None at runtime. Both are load-bearing-looking code a future editor must read and dismiss.

## Fix

Delete both lines. Move the "/* answers print when opened */" note above `details.recall { break-inside: avoid; }` if the behaviour is worth documenting. Net -2 lines.

## Resolution
empty details.recall[open] ruleset and unused .no-print deleted after confirming zero occurrences of no-print in either HTML file
title: Two dead CSS rules
